8. Code Encyclopedia Appendix (Line-by-Line)

1. Project Overview

This product is a tournament operations platform for badminton league matches. It provides referee workflows, live viewer dashboards, standings, finals orchestration, and post-finals category analytics.

Tournament Format

10 round-robin ties (league stage). Each tie has 12 regular games. Game #13 appears only as a decider at 6-6 tie score.

Qualification

After all 10 ties are complete, rank 1 and 2 become finalists. Rank 3 automatically gets Bronze.

Finals

Final tie consists of 12 games between top 2 teams. Winner gets Gold, other finalist gets Silver.

Live Data Model

Viewer and referee screens poll backend frequently; updates propagate without manual refresh.

2. Prerequisites and Local Installation

2.1 Tools Required

  • Python 3.11+ (backend)
  • Node.js 18+ and npm (frontend)
  • Git
  • Optional: Postgres (for production-like local testing)

2.2 Clone and Install

git clone <your-repo-url>
cd badminton-app

# Backend
cd backend
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
python3 seed.py
uvicorn app.main:app --reload

# Frontend (new terminal)
cd ../frontend
npm install
cp .env.example .env
# set VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8000
npm run dev
Result: frontend runs on http://localhost:5173, backend on http://localhost:8000.

2.3 Seed Modes

  • python3 seed.py: fresh tournament start (same teams/players, all pending).
  • python3 seed.py --demo-progress: sample live/completed demo states.

3. How the System Works End-to-End

3.1 Runtime Request Flow

  1. User action in React page (for example, Save Score in Referee).
  2. frontend/src/api.js sends HTTP request.
  3. FastAPI route in backend/app/routes/*.py receives request.
  4. Route calls business logic in backend/app/crud.py.
  5. CRUD updates DB and recalculates tie/final status as needed.
  6. Viewer/Referee polling reloads dashboard and reflects latest state.

3.2 Core Rule Enforcement

  • Referee assignment required before scoring.
  • Lineup confirmation required before scoring.
  • Badminton scoring: 21 with deuce, cap 30.
  • Game #13 only appears/starts when tie is 6-6.
  • Final tie appears only after league completion.

4. Backend Deep Dive (FastAPI + SQLAlchemy)

Backend files analyzed: 18. Total project lines analyzed: 8122.

4.1 Backend Responsibilities

  • Define schema and constraints (models).
  • Expose API contracts (schemas, serializers, routes).
  • Enforce tournament business rules (crud).
  • Initialize default tournament data (seed + startup auto-seed).

5. Frontend Deep Dive (React + Vite)

Frontend files analyzed: 12.

5.1 Frontend Responsibilities

  • Render mobile-first dashboards and score controls.
  • Route between Home, Viewer, Referee, and Post Finals.
  • Poll API frequently to provide near real-time updates.
  • Apply business-guided UX constraints (start/stop/save guards).

6. Deployment Guide

6.1 Single Free Deployment on Vercel (Current Setup)

  1. Import repo into Vercel with root directory as repository root.
  2. Vercel uses vercel.json to build frontend and route API.
  3. Set env vars: VITE_API_URL=/api, DATABASE_URL, CORS_ORIGINS, AUTO_SEED_ON_EMPTY=true.
  4. Deploy and verify /api/health and /api/viewer/dashboard.

6.2 Why /api Rewrite Exists

The project rewrites /api/(.*) to /api?__path=/$1 and then maps __path inside api/index.py. This avoids Vercel nested-path edge NOT_FOUND behavior in some serverless route layouts.

6.3 Optional Split Deployment

  • Frontend on Vercel.
  • Backend on Render using render.yaml.
  • Set frontend VITE_API_URL to Render URL.

7. Operations Runbook

7.1 One-time Full Data Reset

Use carefully: Set AUTO_SEED_FORCE_RESET=true for one deploy/startup to reset tournament state to default seed. Then set it back to false.

7.2 Verification Checklist

  • /api/health returns JSON status ok.
  • Viewer loads without JSON parse errors.
  • Referee can assign and score a match end-to-end.
  • Standings update after completed tie games.
  • Final appears only after all 10 ties completed.

162 key = name.casefold()Variable assignment or state mutation line.
163 if key in excluded_names or key in seen:Conditional branch start.
164 continuePython logic line contributing to backend behavior.
165 seen.add(key)Python logic line contributing to backend behavior.
166 players.append(name)Python logic line contributing to backend behavior.
167 return playersReturns computed value/control from function.

170def _set_option_categories_from_token(token: str) -> list[str]:Function declaration encapsulating a specific unit of backend behavior.
171 text = (token or "").lower()Variable assignment or state mutation line.
172 if " or " not in text:Conditional branch start.
173 return []Returns computed value/control from function.
174 Blank line used for readability and separation of logic blocks.
175 parts = re.split(r"\bor\b", text)Variable assignment or state mutation line.
176 seen: set[str] = set()Variable assignment or state mutation line.
177 categories: list[str] = []Variable assignment or state mutation line.
178 for part in parts:Loop over collection to process each item.
179 number = NoneVariable assignment or state mutation line.
180 match_with_set = re.search(r"set[\s-]*(\d)", part)Variable assignment or state mutation line.
181 if match_with_set:Conditional branch start.
182 number = match_with_set.group(1)Variable assignment or state mutation line.
183 else:Fallback branch when prior conditions are not met.
184 # Supports tokens like "Set 1 OR 2" where second option is a bare number.Comment line explaining intent or a rule.
185 bare_match = re.search(r"\b([1-5])\b", part)Variable assignment or state mutation line.
186 if bare_match:Conditional branch start.
187 number = bare_match.group(1)Variable assignment or state mutation line.
188 if not number:Conditional branch start.
189 continuePython logic line contributing to backend behavior.
190 category = f"Men Set-{number}"Variable assignment or state mutation line.
191 if category in CATEGORY_ORDER and category not in seen:Conditional branch start.
192 seen.add(category)Python logic line contributing to backend behavior.
193 categories.append(category)Python logic line contributing to backend behavior.
194 return categoriesReturns computed value/control from function.
195 Blank line used for readability and separation of logic blocks.
196 Blank line used for readability and separation of logic blocks.

201 if token_category and token_category.startswith("Women"):Conditional branch start.
202 return [token_category]Returns computed value/control from function.
203 if "advance" in lower_token:Conditional branch start.
204 return ["Men Advance"]Returns computed value/control from function.
205 Blank line used for readability and separation of logic blocks.
206 set_options = _set_option_categories_from_token(token)Variable assignment or state mutation line.

209 if token_category:Conditional branch start.
210 return [token_category]Returns computed value/control from function.

214def _category_for_slot_assignment(Function declaration encapsulating a specific unit of backend behavior.
215 token: str,Python logic line contributing to backend behavior.
216 player_name: str,Python logic line contributing to backend behavior.
217 slot_index: int,Python logic line contributing to backend behavior.

219) -> str | None:Python logic line contributing to backend behavior.
220 options = _category_options_from_token(token)Variable assignment or state mutation line.
221 if not options:Conditional branch start.
222 level = set_level_by_name.get(player_name.casefold())Variable assignment or state mutation line.
223 return SET_LEVEL_TO_CATEGORY.get(level) if level else NoneReturns computed value/control from function.
224 Blank line used for readability and separation of logic blocks.
225 if len(options) == 1:Conditional branch start.
226 return options[0]Returns computed value/control from function.
227 Blank line used for readability and separation of logic blocks.
228 # When OR options are present, prefer player's registered set-level.Comment line explaining intent or a rule.
229 level = set_level_by_name.get(player_name.casefold())Variable assignment or state mutation line.
230 if level:Conditional branch start.
231 level_category = SET_LEVEL_TO_CATEGORY.get(level)Variable assignment or state mutation line.
232 if level_category in options:Conditional branch start.
233 return level_categoryReturns computed value/control from function.
234 Blank line used for readability and separation of logic blocks.
235 # Fallback to slot ordering (first lineup name => first option, etc).Comment line explaining intent or a rule.
236 if 0 <= slot_index < len(options):Conditional branch start.
237 return options[slot_index]Returns computed value/control from function.
238 return options[0]Returns computed value/control from function.

226def test_viewer_dashboard_returns_ties_only(client, session_factory):Function declaration encapsulating a specific unit of backend behavior.
